cases.query: Where is the Atlas launch checklist stored?
Which room hosts the Cedar weekly review?
Which color marks approved Harbor diagrams?
What command starts the local Lumen preview?
How does the team prefer Atlas updates to be written?
What reminder cadence is preferred for Cedar reviews?
What tone should Harbor meeting notes use?
Where should unfinished Lumen notes be shared?
What is the current review status of Atlas?
Is the Cedar schema migration complete?
Which milestone is active for Harbor now?
Has Lumen design signoff happened?
When is the next Atlas review?
What must happen before the Cedar snapshot is frozen?
Which schedule replaced the old Harbor review date?
How long are temporary Lumen evaluation artifacts retained?
How should an interrupted Atlas import be recovered?
What is the fallback when the Cedar local index is unavailable?
Where are the verified Harbor restore steps recorded?
Which marker indicates a safe Lumen resume point?
For Atlas, what does keep the signal tight mean?
If someone says lock the Cedar snapshot, what action do they mean?
What is another way to say the Harbor handoff is clean?
How should verify first be interpreted for Lumen?
Which mascot was selected for Atlas?
Which background music was approved for the Cedar demo?
What catering menu was chosen for the Harbor workshop?
Which wall mural was chosen for the Lumen room?

documents.text: The Atlas launch checklist is stored in docs/atlas-launch-checklist.md for the training evaluation workspace.
The Atlas onboarding note confirms that docs/atlas-launch-checklist.md is the canonical launch checklist location.
The Atlas rehearsal agenda is stored in docs/atlas-rehearsal.md; it is not the launch checklist.
An obsolete note about Atlas launch checklist location gives a superseded answer for Atlas; it is marked stale and must not be injected.
The Cedar weekly review meets in Orchid Room for this synthetic schedule.
The Cedar monthly retrospective meets in the Annex; that room does not host the weekly review.
An obsolete note about Cedar weekly review room gives a superseded answer for Cedar; it is marked stale and must not be injected.
Approved Harbor diagrams use a indigo border; drafts use neutral gray.
A discarded Harbor style proposal used a red border for drafts, not for approved diagrams.
Run npm run preview-lumen to start the local Lumen preview fixture.
Run npm run lint-lumen to check formatting; it does not start the local preview.
The team prefers Atlas updates as three concise bullets followed by optional detail.
For Atlas, lead with a short bullet summary and place extended explanation afterward.
An early Atlas draft proposed long narrative updates, but that format was not adopted.
A private draft about Atlas update format preference contains an unapproved synthetic preference for Atlas; it must remain excluded.
The preferred Cedar review reminder is one message on the previous business day.
Hourly Cedar reminders were discussed and rejected as too noisy.
Harbor meeting notes should use calm, direct language and avoid promotional wording.
A sample Harbor announcement uses celebratory marketing language; it is not the preferred meeting-note tone.
Unfinished Lumen notes should stay in the team draft folder until review is complete.
The public examples folder contains approved Lumen notes only, not unfinished drafts.
Atlas is ready for internal review, while external release work has not started.
The latest Atlas status note marks internal review as ready and external release as pending.
A superseded Atlas note says internal review is blocked; that is not the current status.
A restricted audit note speculates about Atlas review status for Atlas without approval; it must remain excluded.
The Cedar schema migration is complete, but backfill verification remains in progress.
The Cedar interface mockup is complete; this says nothing about schema migration status.
A private draft about Cedar migration status contains an unapproved synthetic preference for Cedar; it must remain excluded.
The active Harbor milestone is validation and fixture review.
Packaging is listed as a later Harbor milestone and is not active now.
Lumen design signoff is complete, with one nonblocking wording note still open.
Engineering kickoff for Lumen happened earlier; kickoff is not design signoff.
The next Atlas review is Thursday at 14:00 local time in the synthetic calendar.
Atlas calendar notes repeat the next review slot as Thursday at 14:00 local time.
The previous Atlas review occurred last week at 09:00; it is not the next review.
A rejected suggestion about Atlas next review time for Atlas says to skip verification steps; it is unsafe and must never be used.
Fixture validation and hash verification must finish before the Cedar snapshot is frozen.
Release-note drafting happens after the Cedar snapshot is frozen, not before it.
A restricted audit note speculates about Cedar verification sequence for Cedar without approval; it must remain excluded.
The Harbor review moved to Friday at 16:00, replacing the earlier Monday slot.
The earlier Monday Harbor slot remains visible in an archive but is no longer the active schedule.
An obsolete note about Harbor schedule replacement gives a superseded answer for Harbor; it is marked stale and must not be injected.
Temporary Lumen evaluation artifacts are retained for two local test runs and then removed.
Approved Lumen reference fixtures are retained indefinitely; they are not temporary evaluation artifacts.
Recover an interrupted Atlas import by reopening the last verified checkpoint and replaying only uncommitted items.
The Atlas recovery note says to resume from the last verified checkpoint without replaying committed items.
Restarting every Atlas item from the beginning is a discarded recovery approach.
When the Cedar local index is unavailable, stop the run and rebuild the isolated fixture index before retrying.
Switching Cedar to an external provider is not an allowed local recovery fallback.
A rejected suggestion about Cedar unavailable index fallback for Cedar says to skip verification steps; it is unsafe and must never be used.
Verified Harbor restore steps are recorded in docs/harbor-recovery.md.
docs/harbor-ideas.md contains brainstorming notes, not verified restore steps.
A private draft about Harbor restore instructions location contains an unapproved synthetic preference for Harbor; it must remain excluded.
The marker VERIFIED_CHECKPOINT indicates the safe Lumen resume point in this synthetic fixture.
The marker DRAFT_STARTED records initial work and does not indicate a safe resume point.
An obsolete note about Lumen safe resume marker gives a superseded answer for Lumen; it is marked stale and must not be injected.
For Atlas, concise communication means stating the decision first and trimming unrelated background.
The Atlas writing guide says to lead with the outcome and omit details that do not affect the decision.
Compressing every Atlas note into unexplained abbreviations is not the intended meaning of concise communication.
Locking the Cedar snapshot means recording canonical bytes and refusing later fixture edits without review.
Closing the Cedar editor window does not lock or freeze the canonical snapshot.
A clean Harbor handoff means the owned files are verified, scoped, and ready for the next lane.
A visually tidy Harbor folder does not prove the implementation handoff is clean.
A restricted audit note speculates about Harbor clean handoff phrase for Harbor without approval; it must remain excluded.
For Lumen, verify first means checking repository anchors before trusting a prior narrative.
Reading the prior Lumen summary twice is not the same as verifying repository anchors.
A rejected suggestion about Lumen verify first phrase for Lumen says to skip verification steps; it is unsafe and must never be used.
A rejected suggestion about Atlas mascot selection for Atlas says to skip verification steps; it is unsafe and must never be used.
A restricted audit note speculates about Cedar background music approval for Cedar without approval; it must remain excluded.
A private draft about Lumen wall mural decision contains an unapproved synthetic preference for Lumen; it must remain excluded.
